_readme: Tenant onboarding template. Copy to tenants/<your-tenant-id>.json, fill every
field, then set TENANT_ID=<your-tenant-id> in the environment (Vercel project
env in prod, root .env in dev). No code changes are required per tenant.
Keys prefixed with _ are documentation only and are ignored by lib/tenant.js.
SECURITY: style prompts are served ONLY server-side. /api/config strips them
before anything reaches the browser. Never put secrets (API keys) in here.
_id: URL-safe unique slug. Must equal the filename (without .json) and the TENANT_ID env value.
_businessName: Display name used in the page title, footer, emails, and quote PDF.
_tagline: Short brand line shown under the business name in quotes/emails.
_logo: Path to the logo image, relative to the site root. Drop the file in images/.
_theme: Brand palette + Google Fonts family names. primary/accent drive buttons, links, and highlights; bg is the page background; text is the body color.
_contact: email is shown publicly on the site. notifyEmail receives the lead notification email for every AI visualizer lead (never shown to visitors).
_ui: Tenant-configurable copy. visualizerKicker appears directly above the AI tool.

ui.visualizerKicker: Try it free — see your venue decorated in 60 seconds.

_leadGate: When enabled, the generated render stays blurred until the visitor submits the lead form. fields lists which inputs the form shows (name, email, phone are required by the API; eventDate is optional).
_colors: Balloon color swatches offered to the visitor (max 3 selectable). name is interpolated into the style prompt; hex only drives the swatch UI.
_styles: One entry per offered style. id must be unique per tenant. price is the display estimate (USD). prompt is the server-side image-edit instruction — {colors} is replaced with the visitor's color names. Prompts never reach the browser.

styles.name: Classic Garland
styles.description: Full balloon garland, perfect for entryways and backdrops
styles.prompt: Add a beautiful, full balloon garland arch using the colors {colors} to the main area of this image. Make it look realistic and celebratory. Keep the exact framing, camera angle, and every fixed element of the source photo unchanged — only add the balloon decor.
